research_question: How can a lightweight CNN with fewer than 200K parameters achieve ≥92.5% classification accuracy on FashionMNIST through iterative architectural refinement?
background: FashionMNIST serves as a more challenging drop-in replacement for MNIST, with 10 clothing categories exhibiting higher inter-class similarity (e.g., shirt vs. coat, pullover vs. dress). Designing compact yet accurate models for such tasks is critical for edge deployment and resource-constrained environments. Achieving competitive accuracy under a strict parameter budget requires careful architectural choices such as depthwise separable convolutions, efficient channel allocation, and targeted regularization.

image_analysis.input_characteristics: 28×28 single-channel grayscale images with centered fashion items on a black background, pixel values 0–255
image_analysis.target_characteristics: One of 10 discrete clothing category labels (T-shirt, trouser, pullover, dress, coat, sandal, shirt, sneaker, bag, ankle boot)
image_analysis.visual_gap: Low spatial resolution and grayscale-only input limit fine-grained texture cues; several classes share similar silhouettes (shirt/coat/pullover), requiring the model to capture subtle shape and edge differences

problem_analysis.core_problem: Balancing classification accuracy with extreme parameter efficiency (≤200K) on a dataset where multiple classes have visually similar silhouettes
current_limitations: Naive small CNNs underfit due to insufficient representational capacity for confusable classes
Standard convolutions consume too many parameters at wider channel widths
Simple augmentation and regularization strategies may not sufficiently close the accuracy gap under tight parameter budgets
Most published lightweight architectures (MobileNet, ShuffleNet) are designed for ImageNet-scale inputs and are over-engineered for 28×28 images
problem_analysis.why_difficult: The 28×28 resolution provides very limited spatial information, so the model must extract discriminative features in only 2–3 downsampling stages. Meanwhile, classes like shirt, coat, and pullover differ by only a few pixels in shape, demanding efficient use of every parameter for inter-class discrimination.

in_scope: Custom lightweight CNN architecture design (≤200K parameters)
Depthwise separable and grouped convolutions for parameter efficiency
Lightweight attention mechanisms (SE block, ECA) if parameter-feasible
Standard data augmentation (random crop, horizontal flip, cutout)
Iterative 2–3 cycle improvement with measurable accuracy gains per cycle
Training from scratch on FashionMNIST train/test split
Single-GPU training with short epoch budget (≤50 epochs per cycle)
out_scope: Large pretrained backbones or transfer learning from ImageNet
Neural architecture search (NAS) requiring massive compute
Knowledge distillation from large teacher models
Non-CNN approaches (Vision Transformers, MLPMixers) exceeding parameter budget

primary: compact CNN architecture low parameter budget

quantitative: Training time ≤ 5 minutes per cycle on single GPU
Measurable accuracy improvement across 2–3 iteration cycles
qualitative: Clear architectural rationale for each design choice
Interpretable progression of improvements across cycles
Reproducible results within the automated GitHub pipeline

constraints: Short training time per cycle (suitable for automated loop)
Lightweight CNN-level inference speed (no heavy modules)
No large pretrained models or complex backbones
Must support iterative 2–3 cycle experimental refinement
expected_challenges: Discriminating visually similar classes (shirt vs. coat vs. pullover) with severely limited model capacity, likely requiring targeted architectural choices like channel attention
Avoiding overfitting with a compact model on 60K training samples while still pushing accuracy above 92.5%, demanding careful regularization tuning (dropout, weight decay, augmentation)
Achieving meaningful accuracy gains across each iteration cycle under the tight parameter budget, where naive scaling (more channels/layers) is not an option

input.topic: Lightweight CNN design for FashionMNIST 28x28 grayscale image classification
input.details: Design a lightweight CNN model that achieves high classification accuracy on FashionMNIST while maintaining a small parameter count and fast inference speed. The model should be iteratively improved through 2-3 experiment cycles.
input.problem_definition: Small models lack representational capacity, leading to degraded accuracy. FashionMNIST has higher inter-class visual similarity than MNIST, making simple architectures insufficient. The experiment must also support fast iteration within a GitHub-based automated loop, requiring short training times.
input.desired_outcome: Achieve a validation accuracy of 92.5% or higher with a lightweight classification model. The model should train and infer quickly, with performance progressively improving over 2-3 experimental modification cycles (Path A).
input.constraints: parameter budget ≤ 0.2M, single GPU execution, short training time, lightweight CNN-level inference speed, no overly complex backbones or large pretrained models

retrieval_plan.task_type: Lightweight CNN design for FashionMNIST 28x28 grayscale image classification
